selling_points.feature: Forged carbon steel tines hardened through heat treatment with a solid ash wood handle
selling_points.benefit: Resists bending when penetrating compacted soil, reducing product failures and warranty costs for distributors
selling_points.full_text: Each of the 4 tines is forged from high-carbon steel and heat-treated for optimal hardness, ensuring they hold their shape even in heavy clay soil. The ash wood handle is naturally shock-absorbent and resists cracking under leverage. For buyers, this means lower defect rates and stronger customer loyalty season after season.

selling_points.feature: 0.8 kg balanced weight distribution with a smooth-finished wooden D-grip handle
selling_points.benefit: Minimizes wrist strain during repetitive digging and compost turning, appealing to both DIY gardeners and professional landscapers
selling_points.full_text: The fork is carefully balanced so the weight sits close to the handle, reducing lever fatigue during extended use. The D-grip wooden handle provides a secure, non-slip hold even with sweaty or gloved hands. Retailers benefit from a product that feels premium in hand, increasing the likelihood of in-store conversion.

selling_points.feature: Fully welded socket-to-head construction with a polished, corrosion-resistant finish
selling_points.benefit: Eliminates the weak point where handle meets head, the most common failure area in low-quality forks
selling_points.full_text: Unlike cheap riveted forks that loosen over time, our fully welded socket creates a permanent, one-piece connection between head and handle. The polished steel surface resists surface rust during warehousing and shelf display. Importers appreciate the reduced QC rejection rate and the clean appearance that photographs well for online listings.

selling_points.feature: 4-tine design optimized for soil aeration, compost turning, and root crop harvesting
selling_points.benefit: Serves multiple garden tasks throughout the year, broadening the addressable customer base and extending the retail selling window
selling_points.full_text: The 4-tine configuration is the most versatile fork design — wide enough to move compost efficiently yet narrow enough to aerate lawn soil without tearing grass roots. It doubles as a harvesting tool for potatoes and root vegetables with minimal crop damage. This multi-purpose appeal means your inventory moves across spring, summer, and autumn seasons.

selling_points.feature: Competitive MOQ of 500 pieces with retail-ready color box packaging and 25-day lead time
selling_points.benefit: Allows importers to test the market with a manageable first order while maintaining reliable replenishment cycles
selling_points.full_text: Our standard MOQ of 500 pieces fits comfortably within most container load calculations, and the color box packaging is shelf-ready with barcode space. Consistent 25-day production lead time means you can plan seasonal orders without last-minute air freight premiums. The 0.8 kg unit weight keeps per-unit ocean freight costs competitive.

short_summary: Forged 4-tine carbon steel fork with ash wood handle for versatile soil work.
